name: Tecture IO
description:
Tecture IO is a zero-configuration viewer for file-based C4 architecture diagrams. A developer runs `npx @tecture/core` in any directory that contains an `architecture/` folder (manifest.json + diagrams + node descriptions) and gets an interactive, browser-based exploration tool at http://localhost:3000.

The published npm package is a self-contained Node.js executable that serves both a REST API and the built React UI from the same port. The server is a small Express app that reads the local architecture directory on demand; the browser client is a React + Vite SPA that renders each diagram as an interactive graph using ReactFlow with an ELK-driven layered layout.

There is no database, no authentication, and no deployment surface — the only durable state lives on the user's local filesystem, in the architecture directory that the CLI points at.
